prompt: Create a melancholic monochrome watercolor illustration on textured off-white paper: a fragile young marionette woman seated front-facing with her knees together, torso visible from thighs to head, suspended by exactly six thin vertical puppet strings, three on the left side and three on the right side, extending beyond the top edge. She wears a tattered, lace-like sleeveless dress in washed charcoal gray, with torn floral ruffles around the shoulders, neckline, waist, and skirt hem. Her posture is limp and resigned, shoulders slightly slumped, arms hanging down close to her sides as if controlled by strings. Give her soft ash gray messy short hair gathered loosely into a tousled side bun, with loose strands and indistinct watercolor edges. Cover the face with a flat opaque medium gray square, centered over the facial area, creating an anonymous and unsettling mood. Use delicate ink-like pencil lines for the strings and silhouette, layered gray washes, granulating pigment, blooms, paper stains, dripping watercolor trails, and splattered textures around the body. The background should remain mostly blank white with pale gray wash clouds and vertical drips, emphasizing isolation, control, and the feeling of a marionette who thought she was free. No readable text, no frame, no bright colors, no photorealism.
